rxn_class_summary: Electrode-switchable electrochemical regiodivergent β-azidoiodination (Markovnikov vs anti-Markovnikov) of alkenes with TMSN3 and Bu4NI, where the anode material (Pt vs graphite) controls regiochemistry
electrochemical_role: On graphite anode: I- is oxidized to I2, which forms an iodonium ion with the alkene (polar/cationic Markovnikov path to 2a). On Pt anode: I- is oxidized to hypervalent IO4-, which oxidizes TMSN3 to generate an azide radical that adds to the alkene to give an α-C radical, subsequently trapped by I·/I2 (radical anti-Markovnikov path to 3a).

key_intermediates.name: I2 generated by anodic oxidation of I-
iodonium ion intermediate A from alkene + I2
β-iodo-α-azido cationic intermediate C (Markovnikov, from N3- opening of iodonium)
hypervalent iodine species IO4- formed on Pt anode
azide radical (·N3) from IO4- oxidation of TMSN3
α-carbon radical intermediate B from ·N3 addition to alkene

electron_flow: On graphite: anode oxidizes I- to I2, which forms an iodonium with the alkene; azide opens it at the more substituted carbon to give Markovnikov β-azido-α-iodo product 2a. On Pt: anode oxidizes I- further to hypervalent IO4-, which oxidizes TMSN3 to azide radical; ·N3 adds to alkene producing α-carbon radical B that is captured by I·/I2 to deliver anti-Markovnikov product 3a.

probe_reagents.name: N-allyl amide substrate 4 (radical clock / cyclization probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Under graphite anode standard conditions afforded iodocyclization product 6 (80%), consistent with I2/iodonium pathway; with Pt anode, 6 was not formed (0%), indicating different active iodine species (IOx- on Pt)

probe_reagents.name: NaIO4 (chemical oxidant surrogate for hypervalent iodine)
probe_reagents.observation: Replacing electrolysis with NaIO4 + styrene + TMSN3 gave 3a in 15% yield; with NaIO4 + I2 + Bu4NI gave 3a in 58%, supporting IO4- as the active oxidant on Pt

probe_reagents.name: NaIO3
probe_reagents.observation: Ineffective; no reaction, indicating IO3- is not the operative oxidant

notes: CV showed two oxidation peaks at ~0.58 and 0.86 V (I- → I2 stepwise) on carbon, and an additional higher oxidation at ~2.23 V on Pt that intensified upon TMSN3 addition, supporting formation of hypervalent IO4- on Pt. A minor competing pathway on Pt is direct anodic oxidation of TMSN3 to azide radical, but CV did not show this on graphite. Oxidation of B to carbocation C (radical-polar crossover) cannot be ruled out, but control experiments favor radical trapping of B by I·/I2 to give anti-Markovnikov 3a.
